diseases.name: Malaria
diseases.transmission: Bite of infected female Anopheles mosquito
diseases.india_stats: ~338,000 cases annually (official), estimated ~45,000+ deaths/year (World Malaria Report estimates)

diseases.name: Dengue Fever
diseases.transmission: Bite of infected female Aedes aegypti mosquito
diseases.india_stats: ~100,000 - 200,000 reported cases annually, significant under-reporting in rural areas
diseases.fatality_rate: Under 1% with early care; Severe Dengue (DHF) up to 20% untreated
symptoms: Severe joint and muscle pain ('breakbone fever')

diseases.name: Chikungunya
diseases.transmission: Bite of infected female Aedes aegypti or Aedes albopictus mosquito
diseases.india_stats: ~50,000 - 100,000 cases reported annually across central and southern states
diseases.fatality_rate: Extremely low (<0.1%), but highly debilitating chronic joint pain
symptoms: Severe and persistent joint pain (may last months/years)

diseases.name: Rabies
diseases.transmission: Saliva via bite, scratch, or lick on broken skin of infected mammal
diseases.india_stats: ~20,000 deaths annually (accounts for 36% of global rabies burden)
diseases.fatality_rate: 99.9% (once clinical symptoms appear, it is 100% fatal)
diseases.symptom_onset_days: 20 - 90 days (can vary from 1 week to 1 year)

diseases.name: Leptospirosis
diseases.transmission: Contact of broken skin or mucous membranes with water contaminated by infected rat urine
diseases.india_stats: Endemic in monsoon season in Mumbai, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat
diseases.fatality_rate: 5% - 15% (with pulmonary hemorrhage or Weil's disease up to 50%)

diseases.name: Bubonic Plague
diseases.transmission: Bite of infected Xenopsylla cheopis flea hosted on rats, or direct contact
diseases.india_stats: Historically millions of deaths; currently near-zero cases in India due to sanitation, but monitored

diseases.name: Nipah Virus
diseases.transmission: Direct contact with bats, consumption of date palm sap contaminated with bat saliva/urine, or human-to-human contact
diseases.india_stats: Outbreaks in Kerala (2018, 2021, 2023). 2018 outbreak resulted in 17 deaths out of 18 cases.
